intro: Hedyotis xestosperma, also known as Qingmao, is a plant species belonging to the family Rubiaceae, commonly used in traditional Chinese medicine. The plant has been found to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of fever, sore throat, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Hedyotis xestosperma include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Hedyotis xestosperma is often prepared into teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in combination with other herbs in the form of patent medicines and injectable solutions.
